checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Is each seed in compute_proposer_indices uniquely derived from base seed and slot offset?

reachability.notes: Seed uniqueness ensures each slot gets an independently selected proposer. If seeds collide, the same validator could be repeatedly selected.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Locate the compute_proposer_indices function in the consensus layer codebase. 2. Verify the seed derivation formula: seed_i = hash(base_seed + uint_to_bytes(Slot(start_slot + i))) for each i in [0, SLOTS_PER_EPOCH). 3. Create a test that computes all seeds for a given epoch and verifies no two seeds are identical. 4. Test edge cases: epoch boundaries, maximum slot values, and potential integer overflow in slot calculations. 5. Verify the hash function is cryptographically secure and produces unique outputs for unique inputs. 6. Check that base_seed is properly randomized per epoch to prevent cross-epoch seed collisions.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-34, Source Invariant: FULU-SG004-INV-002, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Primary Element: SG-004

checklist.title: Verify Consensus Layer Proposer Selection Integrity: Can seed collisions bias validator selection across slots?

reachability.notes: Seed uniqueness ensures each slot gets an independently selected proposer. If seeds collide, the same validator could be repeatedly selected.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Analyze how the consensus layer uses seeds from compute_proposer_indices for validator selection. 2. Verify that each slot's proposer is independently determined by its unique seed. 3. Simulate scenarios where seeds might collide (e.g., hash collisions, implementation bugs) and assess impact on proposer distribution. 4. Check for any caching or memoization that might inadvertently reuse seeds. 5. Verify that the validator selection algorithm properly uses the seed to achieve uniform random selection. 6. Assess MEV exploitation potential if an attacker-controlled validator is repeatedly selected.
checklist.notes: Supporting check for actor-consensus-layer node. Source: PROP-W1B10-34, Severity justification: Non-unique seeds would bias proposer selection, potentially assigning the same validator to multiple slots.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the proposer lookahead computed for the correct epoch (N + MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD + 1)?

reachability.notes: Computing proposers for the wrong epoch (e.g., N + MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD instead of N + MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD + 1) would cause proposer assignment errors MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD + 1 epochs later.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify the process_proposer_lookahead function implementation. 2. Trace the epoch calculation logic to verify it uses N + MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD + 1 (not N + MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD or other variants). 3. Verify that the computed proposer indices are placed in the last SLOTS_PER_EPOCH positions of the lookahead array. 4. Create a test case at an epoch boundary and validate the epoch used for new proposer computation. 5. Check for off-by-one errors in the epoch arithmetic. 6. Verify that the correct RANDAO mix is used for the target epoch.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-43, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Invariant: FULU-SG008-INV-002. This is a post-condition that must hold after process_proposer_lookahead completes.

checklist.title: Verify Consensus Layer correctly populates proposer lookahead array positions

reachability.notes: Computing proposers for the wrong epoch (e.g., N + MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD instead of N + MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD + 1) would cause proposer assignment errors MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD + 1 epochs later.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify how the consensus layer invokes proposer lookahead computation. 2. Verify that exactly SLOTS_PER_EPOCH new proposer indices are computed per epoch boundary. 3. Confirm the new indices are appended to the correct array positions (last SLOTS_PER_EPOCH slots). 4. Check that old proposer indices are properly rotated/removed as new ones are added. 5. Verify array bounds are respected to prevent buffer overflows.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-43, Supporting Node: actor-consensus-layer

checklist.title: Verify Validator component receives correct proposer assignments from lookahead

reachability.notes: Computing proposers for the wrong epoch (e.g., N + MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD instead of N + MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD + 1) would cause proposer assignment errors MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD + 1 epochs later.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Trace how validators query the proposer lookahead array for their duties. 2. Verify the slot-to-array-index mapping is correct for the target epoch. 3. Check that validators receive proposer assignments MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD + 1 epochs in advance. 4. Test edge cases at epoch boundaries where the lookahead is being updated. 5. Verify that stale proposer assignments cannot be used after the lookahead is rotated.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-43, Supporting Node: actor-validator

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Does process_proposer_lookahead preserve the exact array length of state.proposer_lookahead?

reachability.notes: If the array grows or shrinks, subsequent index lookups via get_beacon_proposer_index could access out-of-bounds positions or reference stale data.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify the process_proposer_lookahead function implementation. 2. Verify the array length before and after the shift-and-fill operation equals (MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD + 1) * SLOTS_PER_EPOCH. 3. Review all code paths that modify state.proposer_lookahead to ensure no append/pop/resize operations occur. 4. Create a test case that executes process_proposer_lookahead with various initial states and asserts the array length remains constant. 5. Check edge cases: empty slots, maximum validator counts, and boundary epoch transitions.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-44, Source Invariant: FULU-SG008-INV-003, Threat: stride-011, Trust Boundary: tb-009. This is an invariant property - the array length must remain constant after the shift-and-fill operation.

checklist.title: Verify Consensus Layer Node (actor-consensus-layer): Are all callers of get_beacon_proposer_index protected against out-of-bounds access if proposer_lookahead length changes?

reachability.notes: If the array grows or shrinks, subsequent index lookups via get_beacon_proposer_index could access out-of-bounds positions or reference stale data.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Locate all call sites of get_beacon_proposer_index in the consensus layer. 2. Verify each call site handles potential index-out-of-bounds conditions. 3. Check if get_beacon_proposer_index performs bounds checking before array access. 4. Trace the data flow from process_proposer_lookahead output to get_beacon_proposer_index input. 5. Verify no stale data references can occur if the array is modified concurrently.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-44, Supporting check for consensus layer node. Array length invariant violation would cause node crashes or consensus disagreements.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Is the proposer lookahead populated with valid epoch N+1 assignments at epoch N start?

reachability.notes: The deterministic proposer lookahead (EIP-7917) enables validators to prepare for their proposal duties in advance. If the lookahead is not available for N+1 at epoch N, validators cannot prepare.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify the epoch transition handler in the consensus layer implementation. 2. Verify that at the first slot of epoch N, the proposer lookahead data structure contains assignments for all slots in epoch N+1. 3. Trace the computation path that populates the lookahead to ensure it executes deterministically before any validator queries. 4. Test edge cases: epoch 0 transition, epochs following finality gaps, and epochs after validator set changes. 5. Verify that the lookahead computation uses the correct beacon state snapshot (end of epoch N-1).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-45, Source Invariant: FULU-SG008-INV-004, Trust Boundary: tb-009

checklist.title: Verify Consensus Layer correctly computes and exposes proposer lookahead for SG-008

reachability.notes: The deterministic proposer lookahead (EIP-7917) enables validators to prepare for their proposal duties in advance. If the lookahead is not available for N+1 at epoch N, validators cannot prepare.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review the get_beacon_proposer_index or equivalent function to verify deterministic proposer selection. 2. Confirm the lookahead API endpoint returns proposer indices for the next epoch. 3. Verify the computation uses RANDAO from the correct epoch boundary. 4. Check that proposer indices are recalculated correctly when the active validator set changes. 5. Ensure no race conditions exist between epoch transition and lookahead queries.
checklist.notes: Supporting check for actor-consensus-layer node. Source: PROP-W1B10-45

checklist.title: Verify Validator can reliably access proposer lookahead one epoch in advance for SG-008

reachability.notes: The deterministic proposer lookahead (EIP-7917) enables validators to prepare for their proposal duties in advance. If the lookahead is not available for N+1 at epoch N, validators cannot prepare.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Simulate a validator querying proposer duties at the start of epoch N. 2. Verify the response includes all proposer assignments for epoch N+1. 3. Test validator behavior when lookahead is unavailable (error handling). 4. Verify validators can begin block preparation activities based on lookahead data. 5. Confirm timing: lookahead must be available before validators need to act on it.
checklist.notes: Supporting check for actor-validator node. Source: PROP-W1B10-45

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Is the proposer lookahead computation deterministic across all client implementations?

reachability.notes: Cross-client determinism is the fundamental requirement. The proposer lookahead uses hash-based randomness which should be deterministic, but implementation differences in integer handling or seed computation could introduce non-determinism.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Obtain identical beacon state snapshots from multiple client implementations (e.g., Prysm, Lighthouse, Teku, Nimbus, Lodestar). 2. Execute the proposer lookahead computation on each client with the same state input. 3. Compare the resulting proposer_lookahead arrays byte-for-byte across all clients. 4. Check for floating-point operations that could produce platform-dependent results. 5. Verify hash function implementations produce identical outputs for identical inputs. 6. Test sort stability by providing inputs that would expose unstable sorting (equal priority elements). 7. Verify integer overflow/underflow handling is consistent across implementations. 8. Test with edge-case beacon states (empty validator sets, maximum validator counts, boundary epoch values).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-46, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Source Assumption: asm-011, Source Threat: stride-010. Non-deterministic proposer selection would cause different clients to assign different proposers to the same slot, leading to conflicting blocks, missed slots, and potential chain forks.

checklist.title: Supporting Check: Does the consensus layer (actor-consensus-layer) enforce deterministic seed computation for proposer selection?

reachability.notes: Cross-client determinism is the fundamental requirement. The proposer lookahead uses hash-based randomness which should be deterministic, but implementation differences in integer handling or seed computation could introduce non-determinism.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review the RANDAO seed computation logic in the consensus layer. 2. Verify the seed is derived from deterministic inputs only (previous RANDAO reveal, epoch, etc.). 3. Confirm no external entropy sources (system time, random number generators) are used. 4. Trace the seed propagation to the proposer selection algorithm. 5. Verify the shuffling algorithm uses only the deterministic seed. 6. Test that identical seeds produce identical proposer orderings.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-46, Covered Node: actor-consensus-layer. This check ensures the consensus layer correctly implements deterministic seed computation as required by the Ethereum specification.

checklist.title: Supporting Check: Does the validator client (actor-validator) correctly consume and apply the proposer lookahead without introducing non-determinism?

reachability.notes: Cross-client determinism is the fundamental requirement. The proposer lookahead uses hash-based randomness which should be deterministic, but implementation differences in integer handling or seed computation could introduce non-determinism.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review the validator client's handling of the proposer_lookahead array. 2. Verify the validator does not re-compute or modify the lookahead in a non-deterministic way. 3. Check that slot-to-proposer mapping is read directly from the lookahead without transformation. 4. Verify no caching mechanisms could cause stale or inconsistent proposer assignments. 5. Test behavior when the validator receives updated lookahead data (ensure atomic updates). 6. Confirm the validator correctly identifies its own proposal slots using the lookahead.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-46, Covered Node: actor-validator. This check ensures validators correctly consume the proposer lookahead without introducing implementation-specific variations.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is versioned_hashes array correctly computed from blob KZG commitments via kzg_commitment_to_versioned_hash?

reachability.notes: A malicious proposer could include commitments that do not correspond to real blob data. The versioned hash computation and EL-side validation must be correct to prevent this.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify the implementation of kzg_commitment_to_versioned_hash function in the CL codebase. 2. Verify that NewPayloadRequest populates versioned_hashes by iterating over all blob KZG commitments in block body order. 3. Confirm ordering is preserved: index 0 of versioned_hashes corresponds to index 0 of blob commitments. 4. Trace the EL validation path to confirm it recomputes or verifies versioned_hashes against received blob data. 5. Attempt to submit a block with mismatched commitment/versioned_hash pairs and verify rejection. 6. Attempt to submit a block with out-of-order versioned_hashes and verify rejection. 7. Verify error handling when blob data does not match the claimed KZG commitment.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-48, Trust Boundary: tb-006, Threat: stride-012. Critical for data availability guarantees for rollups and L2 systems.

checklist.title: Verify CL-to-EL Trust Boundary: Does the Execution Engine properly validate versioned_hashes received from Consensus Layer?

reachability.notes: A malicious proposer could include commitments that do not correspond to real blob data. The versioned hash computation and EL-side validation must be correct to prevent this.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review the EL's NewPayload handler to identify where versioned_hashes validation occurs. 2. Verify the EL independently validates or cross-checks the versioned_hashes against blob transaction data. 3. Check if the EL trusts CL-provided hashes without verification (potential implicit trust vulnerability). 4. Test with crafted NewPayloadRequest containing valid commitments but corrupted versioned_hashes. 5. Verify the EL returns INVALID status for mismatched hashes. 6. Check for timing side-channels in hash validation that could leak information.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-48, Trust Boundary: tb-006 (CL-EL interface). The EL MUST NOT blindly trust CL-provided versioned_hashes.

checklist.title: Verify Post-Condition: After NewPayload processing, are all blob commitments correctly bound to their versioned hashes?

reachability.notes: A malicious proposer could include commitments that do not correspond to real blob data. The versioned hash computation and EL-side validation must be correct to prevent this.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. For each blob commitment C[i] in a block, verify that versioned_hashes[i] == kzg_commitment_to_versioned_hash(C[i]). 2. Verify the length invariant: len(versioned_hashes) == len(blob_commitments). 3. Check for off-by-one errors in array indexing during hash computation. 4. Verify no truncation or overflow occurs when processing maximum number of blobs per block. 5. Test edge cases: zero blobs, one blob, maximum blobs. 6. Verify the cryptographic binding is correct by checking the hash algorithm implementation matches spec.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-48. Post-condition verification: the mathematical relationship between commitments and versioned hashes must hold after every NewPayload call.

checklist.title: Post-Condition Falsification: Does process_execution_payload correctly update state.latest_execution_payload_header with an accurate ExecutionPayloadHeader?

reachability.notes: If the cached header does not match the payload, subsequent parent_hash checks (PROP-0025) would use incorrect values, breaking execution payload chaining.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify the process_execution_payload function and its success path. 2. Trace the code that derives ExecutionPayloadHeader from the execution payload. 3. Verify field-by-field mapping between the payload and the derived header (block_hash, parent_hash, fee_recipient, state_root, receipts_root, logs_bloom, prev_randao, block_number, gas_limit, gas_used, timestamp, extra_data, base_fee_per_gas, transactions_root, withdrawals_root, blob_gas_used, excess_blob_gas). 4. Create test cases with varied payloads and assert the header fields match exactly after process_execution_payload succeeds. 5. Check for any code paths where the header update could be skipped or partially applied. 6. Verify that subsequent parent_hash checks (PROP-0025) would use the correct cached header values by simulating sequential block processing.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-49, Primary Element: SG-002. This post-condition ensures the cached execution payload header accurately reflects the accepted payload. Failure would break execution payload chaining for subsequent blocks, potentially causing consensus disagreements. Bug bounty eligible - requires PoC showing header caching error and cascading effect on subsequent block validation.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-004/tb-010: Is the fork digest properly validated during P2P block gossip validation?

reachability.notes: A P2P peer can send blocks with arbitrary fork digests. If fork digest validation is missing, blocks from different fork versions could be processed as if they belong to the current fork.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify the gossip validation handler for incoming blocks from P2P peers. 2. Locate the fork digest extraction logic from the received block message. 3. Verify that compute_fork_digest is called with the block's epoch to compute the expected fork digest. 4. Confirm that the received fork digest is compared against the computed expected fork digest. 5. Attempt to send a block with a mismatched fork digest (e.g., from a different fork version) and verify it is rejected with an appropriate error. 6. Test edge cases: fork digest from future epoch, fork digest from past epoch, malformed fork digest bytes.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-50, Trust Boundaries: tb-004, tb-010, Threat: stride-020. This is a pre-condition that must be satisfied before block processing. Failure allows cross-fork block injection.

checklist.title: Verify P2P Peer Block Reception: Does the consensus layer properly reject blocks with invalid fork digests from untrusted peers?

reachability.notes: A P2P peer can send blocks with arbitrary fork digests. If fork digest validation is missing, blocks from different fork versions could be processed as if they belong to the current fork.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Trace the data flow from actor-p2p-peer to actor-consensus-layer for block messages. 2. Verify that fork digest validation occurs BEFORE any state-modifying operations in the consensus layer. 3. Confirm that blocks with mismatched fork digests do not reach state transition logic. 4. Check that peer scoring/banning is applied for repeated invalid fork digest submissions. 5. Verify logging captures fork digest mismatches for monitoring purposes.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-50, Nodes: actor-p2p-peer -> actor-consensus-layer. Supporting check verifying defense in depth between P2P layer and consensus processing.

checklist.title: Verify Fork Digest Computation: Is compute_fork_digest correctly implemented to derive the expected digest for each epoch?

reachability.notes: A P2P peer can send blocks with arbitrary fork digests. If fork digest validation is missing, blocks from different fork versions could be processed as if they belong to the current fork.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Locate the compute_fork_digest function implementation. 2. Verify it takes the block epoch as input and derives the correct fork version for that epoch. 3. Confirm the genesis validators root is correctly incorporated into the digest computation. 4. Test with known test vectors: compute digest for pre-fork epoch, at-fork epoch, and post-fork epoch. 5. Verify the function handles epoch boundary conditions correctly (e.g., exact fork epoch transition).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-50, Element: SG-003. Internal verification that the fork digest computation is correct, as incorrect computation would allow fork confusion attacks.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does the CL implement timeout handling for Engine API calls when EL returns SYNCING status?

reachability.notes: A buggy EL that returns SYNCING indefinitely would stall the CL if no timeout is implemented. Not directly attacker-controlled but could be triggered by triggering EL sync issues via P2P.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify all Engine API call sites in the CL codebase that interact with the EL. 2. Review timeout configuration for each Engine API method (engine_newPayloadV*, engine_forkchoiceUpdatedV*, etc.). 3. Verify that explicit timeout values are set and are reasonable (not indefinite). 4. Test behavior when EL returns SYNCING status repeatedly - confirm CL does not block indefinitely. 5. Verify that other consensus duties (attestations, block proposals) continue to be processed even when Engine API calls are timing out. 6. Check for proper error handling and retry logic when timeouts occur.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-51, Trust Boundaries: tb-006, tb-007, Threat: stride-014

checklist.title: Verify CL resilience: Can the CL continue processing attestations and block proposals while Engine API calls are pending or timing out?

reachability.notes: A buggy EL that returns SYNCING indefinitely would stall the CL if no timeout is implemented. Not directly attacker-controlled but could be triggered by triggering EL sync issues via P2P.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review the CL's task/thread architecture to understand how Engine API calls are dispatched. 2. Verify that Engine API communication is performed asynchronously or in a dedicated thread/task. 3. Check that the main consensus loop is not blocked by Engine API calls. 4. Test by simulating a slow/unresponsive EL and verify attestation duties continue. 5. Verify block production responsibilities are not indefinitely delayed by EL sync issues. 6. Review logging and alerting mechanisms for prolonged SYNCING states.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-51, Covered Actors: actor-consensus-layer, actor-execution-engine

checklist.title: Verify invariant: Extended SYNCING status from EL does not cause CL to miss attestation or proposal duties

reachability.notes: A buggy EL that returns SYNCING indefinitely would stall the CL if no timeout is implemented. Not directly attacker-controlled but could be triggered by triggering EL sync issues via P2P.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Design a test scenario where EL returns SYNCING status for an extended period (e.g., 5+ minutes). 2. Attempt to falsify the invariant by observing whether CL misses any scheduled attestation duties during this period. 3. Attempt to falsify by checking if block proposal opportunities are lost when CL is the designated proposer. 4. Verify state transitions: CL should transition to a degraded operational mode rather than complete stall. 5. Check for any race conditions between Engine API timeout handling and consensus duty scheduling. 6. Verify that once EL returns to VALID/INVALID status, normal operations resume without manual intervention.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-51, Primary Element: SG-002, Type: invariant

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-005/tb-009: Is the proposer deterministically identifiable and cryptographically bound to each slot?

reachability.notes: If the lookahead is incorrect, the wrong validator could be blamed or slashed for actions they did not take.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify that state.proposer_lookahead is computed deterministically from beacon state. 2. Confirm that the proposer lookahead computation is identical across all conforming implementations. 3. Validate that the block proposer signature cryptographically binds the proposer identity to the block. 4. Attempt to craft a block where the proposer signature does not match the expected proposer from the lookahead and verify rejection. 5. Check that lookahead values cannot be manipulated after computation.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-52, Trust Boundaries: tb-005, tb-009, Threat: stride-013

checklist.title: Verify Proposer-Consensus Layer Interaction: Does the consensus layer correctly enforce proposer assignment from the lookahead?

reachability.notes: If the lookahead is incorrect, the wrong validator could be blamed or slashed for actions they did not take.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Trace the data flow from proposer lookahead computation to block proposal acceptance. 2. Verify that the consensus layer rejects blocks from validators not matching the lookahead for a given slot. 3. Check for race conditions or timing issues where lookahead updates could create ambiguity. 4. Confirm that slashing decisions correctly reference the proposer lookahead at the time of the alleged violation.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-52, Node: actor-consensus-layer

checklist.title: Verify Validator Assignment Integrity: Can a validator deny or falsely claim proposer duty assignment?

reachability.notes: If the lookahead is incorrect, the wrong validator could be blamed or slashed for actions they did not take.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify that proposer lookahead is publicly auditable and deterministically reproducible. 2. Check that validators cannot influence their own selection in the lookahead. 3. Confirm that signature requirements prevent a validator from claiming another validator proposed a block. 4. Test that historical proposer assignments are immutable once computed. 5. Verify that any dispute resolution mechanism correctly identifies the assigned proposer.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-52, Nodes: actor-proposer, actor-validator

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Is the MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD delay sufficient to prevent same-epoch randao manipulation of proposer assignments?

reachability.notes: A validator controlling multiple slots could attempt to bias the randao mix to manipulate future proposer assignments. The MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD provides a time buffer, but the attack is theoretically possible with sufficient validator share.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review the implementation of MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD delay in proposer selection. 2. Identify all randao mix contribution points at epoch boundaries. 3. Analyze the proposer lookahead calculation to verify it correctly incorporates the seed lookahead delay. 4. Model the attack surface: calculate the validator stake percentage required to bias randao outcomes. 5. Verify that the delay between randao contribution and proposer assignment prevents same-epoch manipulation. 6. Test edge cases at epoch boundaries where timing assumptions may be violated. 7. Check for any code paths that could allow early revelation or late contribution of randao values.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-53, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Primary Element: SG-006. This is an invariant property covering the consensus layer boundary between validators and the randao system. Bug bounty eligible - requires demonstration that MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD is insufficient with specific validator stake percentage.

checklist.title: Supporting Node Check: Verify validator randao contributions cannot be withheld or replayed to influence proposer selection

reachability.notes: Supporting check for boundary integrity - focuses on validator node behavior.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review how validator randao contributions are collected and validated. 2. Verify that missing randao contributions are handled correctly (e.g., using a default value). 3. Check for replay protection on randao contributions. 4. Verify that the consensus layer properly rejects late or out-of-order randao values. 5. Test scenarios where a validator deliberately withholds their randao contribution to influence the mix.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-53, Supporting check for nodes: actor-validator, actor-consensus-layer

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Are all proposer indices from compute_proposer_indices validated against the active validator set?

reachability.notes: If an inactive validator is selected as proposer, they cannot produce a block, causing a missed slot. The protocol must handle validator exits between lookahead computation and slot execution.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify the compute_proposer_indices function and trace all validator index selection paths. 2. Verify that the function explicitly checks each selected index against the active validator set for the target epoch. 3. Test with edge cases: (a) validator that exits between lookahead computation and slot execution, (b) validator pending activation, (c) slashed validator still in registry. 4. Verify that the epoch parameter used for active set lookup matches the epoch for which proposers are being computed. 5. Check for race conditions where validator status could change during computation. 6. Attempt to construct a scenario where an inactive validator index is returned and verify the system handles this gracefully.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-35, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Source Invariant: FULU-SG004-INV-003, Threat: stride-010

checklist.title: Verify Consensus Layer Correctly Filters Exited Validators from Proposer Selection

reachability.notes: If an inactive validator is selected as proposer, they cannot produce a block, causing a missed slot. The protocol must handle validator exits between lookahead computation and slot execution.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review the consensus layer's get_active_validator_indices function to verify it correctly excludes exited validators. 2. Trace how validator exit_epoch is checked against the current/target epoch. 3. Verify that validators with exit_epoch <= current_epoch are excluded. 4. Test boundary conditions: validator exits exactly at the epoch boundary. 5. Confirm that the active set used for proposer selection is computed atomically with no TOCTOU vulnerabilities.
checklist.notes: Supporting check for actor-consensus-layer. Source: PROP-W1B10-35

checklist.title: Verify Slashed Validators Cannot Be Selected as Block Proposers

reachability.notes: If an inactive validator is selected as proposer, they cannot produce a block, causing a missed slot. The protocol must handle validator exits between lookahead computation and slot execution.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify how the slashed flag is checked during proposer selection. 2. Verify that compute_proposer_indices excludes validators where slashed == true. 3. Test scenario: validator gets slashed between lookahead computation and actual slot. 4. Verify that if a slashed validator somehow produces a block, it is rejected by honest nodes. 5. Check if there are any code paths that could allow a slashed validator to bypass this filter. 6. Review historical slashing events to ensure the exclusion logic is consistent with specification.
checklist.notes: Supporting check for actor-validator. A slashed validator producing blocks could create conflicting blocks. Source: PROP-W1B10-35

checklist.title: Verify Not-Yet-Activated Validators Are Excluded from Proposer Selection

reachability.notes: If an inactive validator is selected as proposer, they cannot produce a block, causing a missed slot. The protocol must handle validator exits between lookahead computation and slot execution.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review how activation_epoch is checked in the proposer selection logic. 2. Verify that validators with activation_epoch > current_epoch are excluded from the active set. 3. Test edge case: validator activates exactly at the epoch for which proposers are being computed. 4. Check that the lookahead computation correctly uses the future epoch's active set, not the current one. 5. Verify no off-by-one errors in epoch comparisons. 6. Test with validators in the activation queue to ensure they cannot be selected prematurely.
checklist.notes: Supporting check for actor-validator. Source: PROP-W1B10-35

Measure CL processing time to assess DoS impact from repeated invalid submissions.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-002: Does the precompile correctly trigger out-of-gas revert when available_gas < 6900?

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify the precompile gas check implementation at the EVM-precompile boundary (tb-002). 2. Call the precompile with exactly 6899 gas and verify it reverts with out-of-gas. 3. Verify that all remaining gas is consumed upon revert (standard EVM out-of-gas semantics). 4. Call with exactly 6900 gas and verify no revert occurs due to gas. 5. Test edge cases: 0 gas, 1 gas, 6898 gas, 6900 gas, 6901 gas. 6. Verify this is the ONLY condition under which the precompile reverts (no other revert paths exist). 7. Compare behavior across different client implementations for consistency.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Construct a transaction calling the precompile with gas < 6900. 2. After the revert, verify that gasUsed equals the gas provided (all gas consumed). 3. Check that no gas is refunded to the caller. 4. Verify consistency with other EVM out-of-gas behaviors (e.g., CALL with insufficient gas). 5. Test from nested call contexts to ensure gas consumption propagates correctly.

checklist.title: Verify Precompile Revert Exclusivity: Does the precompile revert ONLY for gas < 6900?

Test with malformed inputs, edge-case values, and boundary conditions with sufficient gas. 4. Confirm that input validation errors (if any) do NOT cause reverts but are handled per EIP specification. 5. Fuzz the precompile with various inputs while providing >= 6900 gas and verify no reverts.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-008: Is BLOB_SCHEDULE sorted in descending epoch order to ensure correct parameter lookup?

Verify that the schedule is sorted in DESCENDING order by epoch (highest epoch first). 3. Create test cases with multiple epoch entries and confirm the function returns the first entry where epoch >= entry.EPOCH. 4. Attempt to falsify by temporarily changing sort order to ascending and verify that incorrect (outdated) parameters are returned. 5. Compare sort direction implementation across different client implementations to ensure consensus compatibility. 6. Check for any edge cases: empty schedule, single entry, epoch boundary conditions.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-59, Trust Boundary: tb-008, Primary Element: SG-001. This is a consensus-critical implementation detail - all clients must agree on sorting direction.

checklist.title: Verify Blob Schedule Consensus: Do all client implementations use identical sorting direction for BLOB_SCHEDULE lookup?

For each implementation, locate the BLOB_SCHEDULE sorting logic. 3. Verify that ALL implementations use descending (reverse) order by epoch. 4. Create cross-client test vectors: provide identical BLOB_SCHEDULE data and epoch queries, verify identical parameter outputs. 5. Document any discrepancies between clients that could lead to consensus failures.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-59, Supporting node check for actor-blob-schedule and actor-consensus-layer. Inter-client agreement on sorting is essential for network consensus.

checklist.title: Verify Blob Parameter Lookup Logic: Does get_blob_parameters correctly return the first matching entry from descending-sorted schedule?

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-010: Is the compute_fork_digest hashing scheme for blob parameters implemented identically across all client implementations?
