checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is encode_node isinstance dispatch order correct for subclasses?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify any subclass relationships in encoded types. 2. Verify more specific checks come before general checks. 3. Test with new Fusaka transaction types subclassing existing types. 4. Compare isinstance ordering across client implementations. 5. Check for type misrouting causing wrong encoding.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0041, Trust Boundary: tb-001. Wrong dispatch produces different state roots.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is compact encoding leaf/extension distinction preserved in roundtrips?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Encode various leaf and extension nodes. 2. Decode and verify is_leaf flag preserved exactly. 3. Craft Merkle proofs exploiting leaf/extension ambiguity. 4. Test proof verification rejects forged proofs. 5. Check roundtrip consistency for all path lengths.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0042, Trust Boundary: tb-008. Leaf/extension confusion enables state proof forgery.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is root_node type correct at RLP encoding boundary?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create trie producing root_node at RLP size boundary. 2. Verify root_node is Bytes (hash) when RLP >= 32 bytes. 3. Check Root() constructor receives 32-byte hash. 4. Test edge cases at exact boundary. 5. Compare type handling across implementations.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0043, Trust Boundary: tb-003. Incorrect type causes validation failure.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is get_storage_root callback deterministic?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call get_storage_root multiple times for same account. 2. Verify identical Root value returned each time. 3. Test during concurrent storage modifications. 4. Check for caching issues causing inconsistency. 5. Test with Fusaka storage layout changes.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0046, Trust Boundary: tb-006. Non-determinism causes network-wide consensus splits.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is copy_trie safe against mutable value aliasing?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify all trie value objects are immutable/frozen. 2. Check for new Fusaka mutable types in trie. 3. Test modification through copy affecting original. 4. Check during re-org handling and speculative execution. 5. Verify no cross-block state corruption possible.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0047, Trust Boundary: tb-007. Aliasing causes non-deterministic state.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is Osaka account encoding field order correct?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify encode_account includes nonce, balance, storage_root, code_hash in order. 2. Compare encoding with Osaka specification. 3. Test cross-client encoding consistency. 4. Submit account-modifying transactions. 5. Verify identical state roots across implementations.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0048, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-003. Wrong order causes immediate consensus failure.

checklist.title: Falsification Check: Can empty nibble list compact encoding be made incorrect?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Test nibble_list_to_compact([], is_leaf=False) produces 0x00. 2. Test nibble_list_to_compact([], is_leaf=True) produces 0x20. 3. Verify single-byte output with correct parity flag. 4. Test keys matching branch levels exactly. 5. Attempt to produce incorrect encoding at empty boundary.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0049. Empty nibble lists occur at branch boundaries.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is cross-client state root consensus maintained for all Osaka state transitions?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Execute identical transactions on multiple client implementations. 2. Compare computed state roots after each block. 3. Test all Fusaka-specific transaction types. 4. Verify consensus on edge cases (empty trie, boundary conditions). 5. Check for any discrepancy indicating consensus failure.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0050, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002, tb-003, tb-007. Fundamental safety property - chain split is most severe outcome.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is transaction gasLimit > 16777216 rejected at txpool admission?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit a transaction via P2P with gasLimit = 16777217. 2. Verify the transaction is rejected by the txpool. 3. Confirm the transaction does not appear in pending transactions. 4. Test with gasLimit values: 16777216 (accept), 16777217 (reject), 2^32 (reject).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0001, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Invariant: INV-002 (SG-001, EIP-7825)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is GAS_CAP constant exactly 16777216 (2^24)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Inspect the GAS_CAP constant in the client source code. 2. Verify it equals exactly 16777216 (0x1000000). 3. Compare the constant value across all major client implementations (geth, nethermind, besu, erigon). 4. Verify the constant cannot be modified at runtime.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0002, Trust Boundary: tb-007, Invariant: INV-001 (SG-001, EIP-7825)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is tx gas cap independent from block gas limit?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Configure a test network with block_gas_limit > 16777216 (e.g., 30M). 2. Submit a transaction with gasLimit = 20000000 (above tx cap but below block limit). 3. Verify the transaction is rejected at txpool. 4. Verify if somehow included in a block, block validation rejects it.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0003, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-003, Invariant: INV-003 (SG-001, EIP-7825)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does block validation iterate ALL transactions for gas cap check?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Craft a block with N valid transactions followed by 1 oversized transaction. 2. Submit via P2P and verify block is rejected. 3. Craft a block with 1 oversized transaction followed by N valid transactions. 4. Verify block is still rejected (no early-exit optimization). 5. Test with oversized transaction at positions 1, N/2, and N.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0004, Trust Boundary: tb-003, Invariant: INV-005 (SG-002, EIP-7825)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is entire block rejected if ANY transaction exceeds gas cap?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Construct a block with 99 valid transactions and 1 with gasLimit = 16777217. 2. Submit the block and verify complete rejection. 3. Verify no partial acceptance (block should not be processed at all). 4. Check that no state changes occur from the valid transactions.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0005, Trust Boundary: tb-003, Invariant: INV-006 (SG-002, EIP-7825)

checklist.title: Verify Post-Condition: All transactions in valid blocks have gasLimit <= 16777216?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Query recent validated blocks. 2. For each block, verify all transactions have gasLimit <= 16777216. 3. Attempt to inject a block with an oversized transaction and verify it never reaches the execution engine. 4. Monitor execution engine logs for any transaction with gasLimit > cap.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0006, Trust Boundary: tb-004, Post-condition for block validation

checklist.title: Verify Defense-in-Depth: Are both txpool and block validation enforcing gas cap independently?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Disable or bypass txpool validation (if possible in test mode). 2. Verify block validation still catches oversized transactions. 3. Disable or bypass block validation (if possible in test mode). 4. Verify txpool validation still catches oversized transactions. 5. Confirm both layers operate independently.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0007, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-002, tb-003, Invariant: INV-007 (SG-003, EIP-7825)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is cap value 16777216 identical at txpool and block validation?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Audit source code for both txpool and block validation gas cap constants. 2. Verify both reference the same constant or have identical values. 3. Search for any hardcoded values that might diverge. 4. Test boundary: submit tx with gasLimit = 16777216 and verify consistent behavior at both layers.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0008, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-003, Invariant: INV-008 (SG-003, EIP-7825)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Can rejected transactions leak into block building via race conditions?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit oversized transactions while block building is in progress. 2. Use concurrent requests to trigger potential TOCTOU race. 3. Monitor block builder for any inclusion of rejected transactions. 4. Test with high-frequency submission near block boundaries. 5. Review synchronization mechanisms in txpool-to-builder pipeline.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0009, Trust Boundary: tb-002, Invariant: INV-009 (SG-003, EIP-7825)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is gas cap enforced for ALL transaction types (legacy, EIP-2930, EIP-1559, EIP-4844)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit oversized transaction type 0 (legacy) - verify rejection. 2. Submit oversized transaction type 1 (EIP-2930) - verify rejection. 3. Submit oversized transaction type 2 (EIP-1559) - verify rejection. 4. Submit oversized transaction type 3 (EIP-4844 blob) - verify rejection. 5. For each type, test at exactly gasLimit = 16777217.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0010, Trust Boundaries: tb-001, tb-003, Invariant: INV-010 (SG-003, EIP-7825)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is multiplication_complexity >= 16 for all ModExp inputs?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Invoke ModExp with base_length=0, modulus_length=0. 2. Invoke ModExp with base_length=1, modulus_length=1. 3. Invoke ModExp with base_length=32, modulus_length=1. 4. For each, verify multiplication_complexity >= 16. 5. Try to find any input combination producing complexity < 16.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0011, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Invariant: INV-001 (SG-001, EIP-7883)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is multiplication_complexity exactly 16 when max(base_length, modulus_length) <= 32?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Test with base_length=32, modulus_length=32 - expect complexity = 16. 2. Test with base_length=1, modulus_length=32 - expect complexity = 16. 3. Test with base_length=32, modulus_length=1 - expect complexity = 16. 4. Test boundary: base_length=33, modulus_length=32 - expect complexity > 16. 5. Verify exact value of 16 for all small-operand cases.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0012, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Invariant: INV-002 (SG-001, EIP-7883)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is multiplication_complexity = 2 * ceil(max_length/8)^2 for large operands?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Test max_length=33: expected = 2 * ceil(33/8)^2 = 2 * 5^2 = 50. 2. Test max_length=40: expected = 2 * ceil(40/8)^2 = 2 * 5^2 = 50. 3. Test max_length=41: expected = 2 * ceil(41/8)^2 = 2 * 6^2 = 72. 4. Verify ceiling division (not floor). 5. Test non-multiples of 8: 33, 34, 39, 41, 47, 49.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0013, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Invariant: INV-003 (SG-001, EIP-7883)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is iteration_count >= 1 for all non-trivial ModExp inputs?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Invoke ModExp with exponent=0, exponent_length=1. 2. Invoke ModExp with exponent=0, exponent_length=32. 3. Verify iteration_count = max(calculated_value, 1) = 1. 4. Test edge cases that might produce iteration_count = 0 before the max() clamp. 5. Confirm no zero-cost execution path exists.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0014, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Invariant: INV-001 (SG-002, EIP-7883)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is iteration_count exactly 1 for zero exponent with length <= 32?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Invoke ModExp with exponent=0, exponent_length=1. 2. Verify iteration_count = 1 (not 0, not > 1). 3. Test with exponent=0, exponent_length=32. 4. Verify iteration_count = 1. 5. Compare gas costs to ensure correct pricing for zero exponent.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0015, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Invariant: INV-002 (SG-002, EIP-7883)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is large exponent multiplier 16 (not 8) in iteration_count formula?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Test with exponent_length=33, top_32_bytes with bit_length=1. 2. Expected: iteration_count = 16 * (33-32) + 1 = 17. 3. Old formula would give: 8 * (33-32) + 1 = 9. 4. Verify actual gas cost matches EIP-7883 (multiplier 16). 5. Test across multiple clients for consistency.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0016, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Invariant: INV-003 (SG-002, EIP-7883)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp gas cost >= 500 for all inputs?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Invoke ModExp with minimal inputs (base_length=1, modulus_length=1, exponent_length=1, exponent=1). 2. Verify gas cost = max(500, computed_cost). 3. Test old minimum of 200 is NOT used. 4. Try various small-input combinations to find minimum. 5. Confirm 500 is the absolute floor.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0017, Trust Boundaries: tb-005, tb-006, Invariant: INV-001 (SG-003, EIP-7883)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does ModExp gas formula NOT include /3 divisor?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Compute gas for a known input using EIP-7883 formula. 2. Compute gas using EIP-2565 formula (with /3). 3. Invoke ModExp with that input and measure actual gas. 4. Verify actual matches EIP-7883 (no /3), NOT EIP-2565. 5. Expected: EIP-7883 gas ~= 3 * EIP-2565 gas (roughly).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0018, Trust Boundary: tb-006, Invariant: INV-002 (SG-003, EIP-7883)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp gas computation deterministic across all clients?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create a test vector of 100+ diverse ModExp inputs. 2. Execute each on geth, nethermind, besu, erigon. 3. Compare gas costs for identical inputs across clients. 4. Any divergence indicates potential consensus bug. 5. Focus on edge cases: boundary values, non-multiples of 8, large exponents.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0019, Trust Boundary: tb-006, Consensus-critical

checklist.title: Verify Pre-Condition: Does tx.gasLimit > 16777216 trigger rejection path?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with gasLimit = 16777217. 2. Trace code execution to verify rejection branch is taken. 3. Confirm transaction is not added to txpool. 4. Verify appropriate error is returned. 5. Test state machine transition: q_check -> q_reject.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0020, Trust Boundary: tb-001, State transition: q_check -> q_reject (SG-001)

checklist.title: Verify Pre-Condition: Does tx.gasLimit <= 16777216 trigger acceptance path?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with gasLimit = 16777216 (exactly at cap). 2. Verify transaction is accepted into txpool. 3. Submit transaction with gasLimit = 16777215. 4. Verify transaction is accepted. 5. Test state machine transition: q_check -> q_accept.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0021, Trust Boundary: tb-001, State transition: q_check -> q_accept (SG-001)

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does block validation catch txpool-bypassing oversized transactions?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Simulate private transaction channel bypassing txpool. 2. Construct block with oversized transaction. 3. Submit block via P2P to honest validator. 4. Verify block is rejected at block validation layer. 5. Test with MEV-Boost simulation if applicable.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0022, Trust Boundary: tb-008, Defense-in-depth validation

checklist.title: Verify Boundary Condition: Does txpool use strict comparison (> not >=) for gas cap?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit tx with gasLimit = 16777215 - expect accept. 2. Submit tx with gasLimit = 16777216 - expect accept. 3. Submit tx with gasLimit = 16777217 - expect reject. 4. Audit source code for comparison operator. 5. Confirm >(gasLimit, cap) not >=(gasLimit, cap).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0023, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Boundary condition at SG-001

checklist.title: Verify Boundary Condition: Does block validation use identical comparison to txpool?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Audit block validation code for comparison operator. 2. Confirm identical to txpool (> not >=). 3. Test block with tx gasLimit = 16777216 - should be valid. 4. Test block with tx gasLimit = 16777217 - should be invalid. 5. Cross-check both layers for consistency.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0024, Trust Boundary: tb-003, Boundary condition at SG-002

checklist.title: Verify Post-Condition: Is multiplication_complexity = 16 when max_length <= 32?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Trace state transition q_max -> q_small for max_length=32. 2. Verify pre-condition: max_length <= 32. 3. Verify post-condition: complexity == 16. 4. Test boundary: max_length=32 (small path) vs max_length=33 (large path). 5. Confirm correct branch selection.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0025, Trust Boundary: tb-005, State: q_max -> q_small (SG-001, EIP-7883)

checklist.title: Verify Post-Condition: Is multiplication_complexity = 2*ceil(max_length/8)^2 when max_length > 32?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Trace state transition q_max -> q_large for max_length=33. 2. Verify pre-condition: max_length > 32. 3. Verify post-condition: complexity = 2 * ceil(33/8)^2 = 50. 4. Test with max_length values 33, 64, 128, 256. 5. Verify formula matches specification exactly.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0026, Trust Boundary: tb-005, State: q_max -> q_large (SG-001, EIP-7883)

checklist.title: Verify Post-Condition: Is iteration_count using multiplier 16 for exponent_length > 32?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Trace q_large -> q_compute_large transition for exponent_length=64. 2. Verify formula: 16 * (64-32) + bit_length(top_32). 3. Compare with old formula: 8 * (64-32) + bit_length. 4. Confirm 2x difference in iteration_count. 5. Verify across multiple client implementations.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0027, Trust Boundary: tb-005, State: q_large -> q_compute_large (SG-002, EIP-7883)

checklist.title: Is rate-limiting effective against cap-sized transaction flooding?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit many transactions with gasLimit = 16777216 from single sender. 2. Verify per-sender limits are enforced. 3. Measure node performance under transaction flood. 4. Verify EIP-1559 base fee provides economic rate limiting. 5. Test txpool eviction policies for cap-sized transactions.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0028, Trust Boundary: tb-001, STRIDE-Spoofing-tb-001

checklist.title: Verify Defense-in-Depth: Does block validation catch compromised txpool leaks?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Simulate compromised txpool allowing oversized transactions. 2. Block builder includes such transaction. 3. Submit resulting block to honest validators. 4. Verify all honest validators reject the block. 5. Confirm defense-in-depth model is functional.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0029, Trust Boundaries: tb-002, tb-003, STRIDE-Tampering-tb-002

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do all honest validators reject malicious builder blocks?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Construct block with malicious builder containing oversized tx. 2. Broadcast to network of honest validators. 3. Verify 100% rejection rate. 4. Test on diverse client implementations. 5. Monitor for any validator accepting the invalid block.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0030, Trust Boundary: tb-003, STRIDE-Tampering-tb-003

checklist.title: Is number of cap-sized transactions per block bounded by block_gas_limit / 16777216?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Calculate floor(30000000 / 16777216) = 1 for typical 30M block limit. 2. Attempt to include 2 cap-sized transactions in one block. 3. Verify block is rejected due to exceeding block gas limit. 4. Confirm natural bound is enforced. 5. This is by-design behavior, not a vulnerability.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0031, Trust Boundary: tb-003, STRIDE-DoS-tb-003

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp charged >= 500 gas for all edge case inputs?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Invoke ModExp with base_length=0, modulus_length=0, exponent_length=0. 2. Invoke ModExp with base_length=0, modulus_length=0, exponent_length=1, exponent=0. 3. Invoke ModExp with maximum possible input lengths. 4. Verify all cases charge at least 500 gas. 5. Look for any input bypassing the minimum floor.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0033, Trust Boundary: tb-005, STRIDE-Tampering-tb-005

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is gas cost proportional to actual computation for ModExp?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Benchmark ModExp execution times for various inputs. 2. Compare wall-clock time to gas charged. 3. Look for inputs where time/gas ratio is unusually high. 4. Test inputs known to be expensive (large exponents). 5. Verify no cheap-but-slow computation paths exist.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0034, Trust Boundary: tb-005, STRIDE-DoS-tb-005, Core EIP-7883 security goal

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp gas identical across all client implementations?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create comprehensive ModExp test vector suite. 2. Execute on geth, nethermind, besu, erigon, reth. 3. Compare gas values for each test case. 4. Focus on boundary values and edge cases. 5. Any divergence = consensus-critical bug.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0035, Trust Boundary: tb-006, STRIDE-Tampering-tb-006

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ModExp gas calculation safe from integer overflow?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Test with maximum possible base_length, modulus_length, exponent_length. 2. Calculate intermediate values (complexity * iterations). 3. Verify no overflow in 64-bit, 128-bit, or 256-bit arithmetic. 4. Check client source for appropriate integer types. 5. Test near-overflow values to trigger potential wrap-around.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0036, Trust Boundary: tb-006, STRIDE-EoP-tb-006

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are EIP-7825 and EIP-7883 activated simultaneously at Fusaka fork?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Check fork configuration for activation block/timestamp. 2. Verify EIP-7825 and EIP-7883 have identical activation. 3. Compare activation across all client implementations. 4. Test behavior at fork boundary (block N-1, N, N+1). 5. Any activation difference = consensus split risk.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0037, Trust Boundary: tb-007, STRIDE-Tampering-tb-007. Requires further investigation due to conditional scope.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Do MEV-Boost relays validate gas cap for submitted blocks?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit block with oversized transaction to MEV-Boost relay. 2. Verify relay rejects the block. 3. Test with multiple relay implementations. 4. Verify proposer also rejects if relay somehow passes it. 5. Test end-to-end: malicious builder -> relay -> proposer.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0039, Trust Boundary: tb-008, STRIDE-Spoofing-tb-008

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does block validation catch private channel txpool bypasses?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Simulate Flashbots Protect-style private channel. 2. Submit oversized transaction through private channel. 3. Include in block and submit to validators. 4. Verify block validation catches violation. 5. Confirm no bypass path exists for private transactions.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0040, Trust Boundary: tb-008, STRIDE-Tampering-tb-008

checklist.title: Verify Pre-Condition: Is ceiling division used in multiplication_complexity for words?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Test max_length=33: words = ceil(33/8) = 5 (not floor = 4). 2. Test max_length=39: words = ceil(39/8) = 5. 3. Test max_length=40: words = ceil(40/8) = 5. 4. Test max_length=41: words = ceil(41/8) = 6. 5. Verify ceiling, not floor division throughout.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0042, Trust Boundary: tb-005, Pre-condition for q_large -> q_compute

checklist.title: Verify Post-Condition: Is iteration_count = bit_length(exponent) - 1 for small non-zero exponents?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Test exponent=1 (bit_length=1): iteration_count = 0, but max(0,1) = 1. 2. Test exponent=2 (bit_length=2): iteration_count = 1. 3. Test exponent=128 (bit_length=8): iteration_count = 7. 4. Test exponent=255 (bit_length=8): iteration_count = 7. 5. Verify bit_length computation is correct.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0043, Trust Boundary: tb-005, State: q_nonzero -> q_final (SG-002, EIP-7883)

checklist.title: Verify Post-Condition: Is bit_length of top 32 bytes computed correctly for large exponents?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Test exponent with leading zeros in top 32 bytes. 2. Verify bit_length only counts significant bits. 3. Test top_32_bytes = 0x00...01 (bit_length = 1). 4. Test top_32_bytes = 0x80...00 (bit_length = 256). 5. Verify correct extraction and bit counting.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0044, Trust Boundary: tb-005, State: q_large -> q_compute_large (SG-002, EIP-7883)

checklist.title: Verify Invariant: Is ModExp gas computation using only integer arithmetic (no floating-point)?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Audit client source code for ModExp gas calculation. 2. Verify no float/double types used. 3. Check all intermediate values are integer. 4. Verify floor() is on integer result, not float conversion. 5. Test on different hardware platforms for determinism.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0045, Trust Boundary: tb-006, Consensus-critical

checklist.title: Verify Pre-Condition: Is gas cap check on raw tx.gasLimit, not derived values?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Audit txpool code for gas cap check location. 2. Verify check is on raw field, not after any transformation. 3. Test with gasLimit values near 2^32 boundary. 4. Test with gasLimit values near 2^64 boundary. 5. Verify no truncation before comparison.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0047, Trust Boundary: tb-001, State: q_receive -> q_check (SG-001)

checklist.title: Verify Invariant: Is ModExp gas cost monotonically non-decreasing with input size?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. For base_length = 1 to 1000, verify gas(n) <= gas(n+1). 2. For modulus_length = 1 to 1000, verify monotonicity. 3. For exponent_length = 1 to 1000, verify monotonicity. 4. For exponent value increasing, verify monotonicity. 5. Any decrease indicates formula bug.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0049, Trust Boundary: tb-005

checklist.title: Verify Invariant: Is EIP-7883 gas cost >= EIP-2565 gas cost for all inputs?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create comprehensive test vector of ModExp inputs. 2. Compute EIP-2565 gas for each input. 3. Compute EIP-7883 gas for each input. 4. Verify EIP-7883 >= EIP-2565 for all cases. 5. Any regression indicates implementation bug.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0050, Trust Boundaries: tb-005, tb-006

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ECDSA signature recovery correctly deriving sender addresses?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review recover_sender implementation for correct secp256k1_recover usage. 2. Verify keccak256 hash is correctly applied to recovered public key. 3. Test with malformed r/s/v values to ensure no sender address spoofing. 4. Attempt to craft a transaction that passes validation but returns wrong sender address. 5. Verify address derivation uses last 20 bytes of keccak256 hash.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0001. Boundary: tb-001, tb-009. Critical severity - signature forgery would enable complete account impersonation.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is the ECDSA r-component range validation enforced?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify where r validation occurs in recover_sender. 2. Submit transaction with r == 0 and verify rejection. 3. Submit transaction with r == SECP256K1N and verify rejection. 4. Submit transaction with r == SECP256K1N - 1 and verify acceptance. 5. Check that rejection happens before any state access.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0002. Boundary: tb-001, tb-009. Pre-condition requiring 0 < r < SECP256K1N.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is EIP-2 low-s normalization enforced?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with s == 0 and verify rejection. 2. Submit transaction with s > SECP256K1N / 2 and verify rejection. 3. Submit transaction with s == SECP256K1N / 2 and verify acceptance. 4. Attempt transaction malleability by flipping s to SECP256K1N - s. 5. Verify both transaction variants cannot coexist in mempool.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0003. Boundary: tb-001, tb-009. High-s enables tx malleability per EIP-2.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are transaction type signing hashes non-colliding?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Enumerate all transaction types (legacy, 0x01, 0x02, 0x03, 0x04). 2. Verify each type has unique prefix bytes. 3. Attempt to find field values that produce colliding signing hashes across types. 4. Verify type prefixes do not overlap with valid RLP first bytes for legacy. 5. Check that a signature from one type cannot be reused for another type.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0004. Boundary: tb-009. Cross-type signature confusion would change transaction semantics.

checklist.title: Verify Conditional Scope: Pre-EIP-155 cross-chain replay vulnerability awareness
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify that v == 27 or v == 28 indicates pre-EIP-155 transaction. 2. Check if client defaults to pre-155 signing under any circumstances. 3. Verify warnings are displayed when handling pre-155 transactions. 4. Document any Fusaka-specific changes to pre-155 behavior. 5. Check that pre-155 transactions are still valid but flagged appropriately.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0005. CONDITIONAL SCOPE - Known design limitation. Only in-scope if Fusaka changes alter pre-155 behavior.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does snapshot mechanism provide true deep-copy isolation?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review copy_trie implementation for deep vs shallow copy. 2. Create nested snapshots via contract calls. 3. Mutate state in inner call and verify outer snapshot remains unaffected. 4. Test rollback_transaction restores exact state from begin_transaction. 5. Check both _main_trie and _storage_tries are properly copied.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0006. Boundary: tb-003. Shallow copy would break transaction atomicity.

checklist.title: Is the snapshot count invariant maintained after begin_transaction?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Record len(state._snapshots) before begin_transaction. 2. Call begin_transaction and verify len(state._snapshots) increased by exactly 1. 3. Test nested begin_transaction calls maintain correct count. 4. Verify no snapshot count changes occur from concurrent operations. 5. Test edge cases with maximum nesting depth.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0007. Post-condition ensuring snapshot lifecycle integrity.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does commit_transaction correctly decrement snapshot count?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Record len(state._snapshots) before commit_transaction. 2. Call commit_transaction and verify len decreased by exactly 1. 3. Verify transient_storage._snapshots also decreases by 1. 4. Test complex call patterns that exercise commit under adversarial conditions. 5. Check for off-by-one errors in snapshot popping.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0008. Boundary: tb-003. Incorrect snapshot management could persist reverted state.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is created_accounts cleared only at outermost snapshot pop?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create nested transactions with account creation in inner call. 2. Commit inner transaction and verify created_accounts NOT cleared. 3. Commit outermost transaction and verify created_accounts IS cleared. 4. Test gas refund calculations depend on correct created_accounts state. 5. Verify EIP-6780 SELFDESTRUCT eligibility is correctly tracked.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0009. Boundary: tb-003. Affects EIP-2200 gas accounting and EIP-6780 constraints.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does rollback_transaction restore exact snapshot state?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Record complete state at begin_transaction. 2. Make multiple state modifications. 3. Trigger rollback_transaction via REVERT or out-of-gas. 4. Compare post-rollback state with recorded pre-modification state. 5. Verify both _main_trie and _storage_tries are identical to snapshot.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0010. Boundary: tb-003. Incomplete rollback breaks EVM atomicity guarantee.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is transient storage correctly restored after rollback?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Set transient storage values before sub-call. 2. Modify transient storage in sub-call. 3. Trigger REVERT in sub-call. 4. Verify transient storage values match pre-sub-call state. 5. Test re-entrancy guard patterns using TSTORE-based locks.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0011. Boundary: tb-003. Transient storage desync could break re-entrancy guards (EIP-1153).

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are persistent and transient snapshot stacks synchronized?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify len(state._snapshots) == len(transient_storage._snapshots) at all times. 2. Test commit_transaction pops from both stacks atomically. 3. Test rollback_transaction pops from both stacks atomically. 4. Simulate exception during commit/rollback and verify no desync. 5. Check recovery behavior if stacks become desynchronized.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0012. Boundary: tb-003. Stack desync causes wrong rollback restoration.

checklist.title: Is get_account guaranteed to never return None?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call get_account for nonexistent addresses and verify EMPTY_ACCOUNT returned. 2. Verify get_account never returns None under any input. 3. Test with addresses that have been destroyed. 4. Test with addresses at state trie boundaries. 5. Verify downstream functions handle EMPTY_ACCOUNT correctly.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0013. Invariant - None return would cause NoneType errors in state operations.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does set_account(None) preserve storage?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create account with storage. 2. Call set_account(addr, None). 3. Verify account removed from _main_trie. 4. Verify storage trie still exists in _storage_tries. 5. Document any code paths that call set_account(None) directly.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0014. Boundary: tb-005. Orphaned storage could be inherited by new accounts.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does destroy_account remove both account and storage atomically?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create account with storage. 2. Trigger SELFDESTRUCT (EIP-6780 constraints permitting). 3. Verify account removed from _main_trie. 4. Verify address NOT in _storage_tries. 5. Deploy new contract at same address and verify no storage inheritance.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0015. Boundary: tb-005. Incomplete destruction enables unauthorized storage access.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does destroy_storage handle double-deletion safely?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call destroy_storage on address with storage - verify deletion succeeds. 2. Call destroy_storage on address without storage - verify no-op (no KeyError). 3. Call destroy_storage twice on same address - verify second is no-op. 4. Verify no exception propagates from double deletion. 5. Test under SELFDESTRUCT trigger conditions.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0016. Boundary: tb-005. KeyError during destruction would crash node.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does mark_account_created persist across reverts?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create account in sub-call via CREATE/CREATE2. 2. Revert the sub-call. 3. Verify address still in created_accounts. 4. Verify SSTORE gas accounting reflects created status. 5. Verify marker only cleared at outermost transaction commit.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0017. Boundary: tb-005. Affects EIP-2200 gas refunds and EIP-6780 SELFDESTRUCT eligibility.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does get_storage return U256(0) for unset keys?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call get_storage on address without storage trie - verify U256(0). 2. Call get_storage on address with trie but unset key - verify U256(0). 3. Verify SLOAD opcode returns 0 for never-written slots. 4. Test at storage key boundaries (0x0, max uint256). 5. Test for destroyed accounts.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0018. Boundary: tb-005. Non-zero default would cause widespread contract execution errors.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does set_storage require account existence?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Attempt SSTORE to destroyed account within same transaction. 2. Verify AssertionError is raised before storage write. 3. Verify no orphaned storage trie is created. 4. Test via contract that SELFDESTRUCTs then tries SSTORE. 5. Verify state trie consistency after rejection.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0019. Boundary: tb-005. Orphaned storage tries would corrupt state.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are empty storage tries cleaned up?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Write storage value to new address. 2. Zero out the storage value. 3. Verify address NOT in _storage_tries after cleanup. 4. Write to same address again and verify new trie created. 5. Test memory growth from repeated write/zero patterns.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0020. Boundary: tb-005. Missing cleanup causes memory accumulation.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is storage_root blocked during active transaction?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Attempt storage_root computation with active snapshots. 2. Verify AssertionError is raised. 3. Verify assertion checks 'not state._snapshots'. 4. Test that block production code never calls storage_root mid-transaction. 5. Verify consensus code waits for transaction completion.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0021. Boundary: tb-010. Incorrect root during active tx causes consensus failure.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does state_root incorporate all storage roots?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify get_storage_root called for every account in _main_trie. 2. Verify correct root returned for accounts with storage. 3. Verify EMPTY_TRIE_ROOT returned for accounts without storage. 4. Compare computed state_root against reference implementation. 5. Test with large number of accounts with varying storage sizes.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0022. Boundary: tb-010. Missing/incorrect storage roots cause consensus split.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does account_has_storage check key existence only?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create account with storage trie containing entries. 2. Verify account_has_storage returns True. 3. Zero out all entries (empty trie). 4. Check if empty trie persists (depends on cleanup). 5. Verify return value matches key existence, not entry count.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0023. Boundary: tb-005. Known limitation - may return false positive for empty tries.

checklist.title: Is account liveness check correctly implemented?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify is_account_alive returns False for nonexistent address. 2. Verify is_account_alive returns False for EMPTY_ACCOUNT. 3. Verify is_account_alive returns True for any non-empty account. 4. Test EIP-161 empty account cleanup integration. 5. Verify liveness affects correct downstream operations.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0024. Incorrect liveness could prevent cleanup or destroy live accounts.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does modify_state destroy empty accounts?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create account with balance, transfer out all balance. 2. Verify account has nonce==0, code==b'', balance==0. 3. Verify destroy_account is called automatically. 4. Verify account removed from _main_trie. 5. Test all code paths through modify_state that could result in empty accounts.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0025. Boundary: tb-003, tb-005. State bloat and ghost accounts if cleanup fails.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ether conservation maintained in move_ether?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Record sender.balance + recipient.balance before move_ether. 2. Execute move_ether with various amounts. 3. Verify sum of balances unchanged after operation. 4. Test with maximum balance values to check overflow. 5. Verify atomic update of both balances.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0026. Boundary: tb-003, tb-005. Ether creation/destruction is catastrophic protocol violation.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does move_ether check sufficient balance?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Attempt move_ether with amount > sender.balance. 2. Verify AssertionError is raised. 3. Verify state is unchanged after rejection. 4. Test at balance boundaries (sender.balance == amount). 5. Verify no partial transfer occurs.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0027. Boundary: tb-003. Missing check enables unlimited ether extraction.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does increment_nonce increase by exactly 1?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Record nonce before increment_nonce. 2. Call increment_nonce and verify nonce == old_nonce + 1. 3. Test at U64.MAX - 1 boundary. 4. Verify wrapping behavior at U64.MAX (should not wrap). 5. Verify nonce is monotonically increasing.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0028. Boundary: tb-003. Nonce wrap-around enables replay of historical transactions.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does get_storage_original return U256(0) for created accounts?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create new account within transaction. 2. Call get_storage_original for any key. 3. Verify U256(0) returned regardless of current storage. 4. Verify SSTORE gas accounting reflects zero original value. 5. Test EIP-2200 gas refund calculations.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0029. Boundary: tb-005. Non-zero original for created accounts causes wrong gas refunds.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does get_storage_original read from outermost snapshot?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Record storage value before transaction. 2. Modify storage in transaction. 3. Call get_storage_original and verify it matches pre-tx value. 4. Verify _snapshots[0] is used, not current state. 5. Test in deeply nested call contexts.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0030. Boundary: tb-005. Wrong snapshot index causes incorrect EIP-2200 gas refunds.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does transient storage return U256(0) for unset keys?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call get_transient_storage for address without transient trie. 2. Verify U256(0) returned. 3. Call for address with trie but unset key. 4. Verify U256(0) returned. 5. Test TLOAD opcode returns 0 for never-written slots.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0031. Boundary: tb-005. Non-zero default could break re-entrancy guards using EIP-1153.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is transient storage properly scoped to single transaction?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Set transient storage in transaction A. 2. Commit transaction A. 3. Start new transaction B. 4. Read transient storage and verify all values are U256(0). 5. Verify transient_storage._tries is empty at transaction start.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0032. Boundary: tb-003, tb-005. Cross-tx leakage breaks all TSTORE/TLOAD contracts.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are empty transient storage tries cleaned up?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. TSTORE a value to new address. 2. TSTORE zero to same slot. 3. Verify address NOT in transient_storage._tries. 4. Test memory growth within single transaction. 5. Verify cleanup bounded by gas limit.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0033. Boundary: tb-005. Memory growth bounded by tx gas limit.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is EIP-7623 gas validation enforced correctly?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Compute intrinsic_gas and calldata_floor for test transaction. 2. Submit tx with gas == max(intrinsic_gas, calldata_floor) - 1. 3. Verify InsufficientTransactionGasError raised. 4. Submit tx with gas == max(intrinsic_gas, calldata_floor). 5. Verify transaction accepted. Check for off-by-one in comparison.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0034. Boundary: tb-001, tb-002. Fusaka-specific EIP-7623 validation.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is EIP-2681 nonce overflow check enforced?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with nonce == U64.MAX_VALUE. 2. Verify NonceOverflowError raised. 3. Submit transaction with nonce == U64.MAX_VALUE - 1. 4. Verify transaction accepted (if other checks pass). 5. Verify U256 comparison used, not U64 (no wrap).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0035. Boundary: tb-001, tb-002. Overflow to 0 enables historical tx replay.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is MAX_INIT_CODE_SIZE enforced for contract creation?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit contract creation tx with len(data) == MAX_INIT_CODE_SIZE + 1. 2. Verify InitCodeTooLargeError raised. 3. Submit with len(data) == MAX_INIT_CODE_SIZE. 4. Verify transaction accepted. 5. Verify check only applies when tx.to == Bytes0.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0036. Boundary: tb-001, tb-002. Oversized initcode causes DoS during deployment.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is EIP-7825 TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT enforced?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Submit transaction with gas == 16,777,217 (TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT + 1). 2. Verify TransactionGasLimitExceededError raised. 3. Submit with gas == 16,777,216. 4. Verify transaction accepted. 5. Compare behavior across client implementations for consensus.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0037. Boundary: tb-001, tb-002. Fusaka-specific EIP-7825. Cross-client divergence causes consensus failure.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is EIP-7623 calldata token counting correct?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create calldata with known zero/non-zero byte distribution. 2. Compute expected tokens: zero_count*1 + non_zero_count*4. 3. Verify implementation matches expected token count. 4. Test edge cases: all zeros, all non-zeros, mixed patterns. 5. Verify both intrinsic_gas and calldata_floor use correct counts.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0038. Boundary: tb-001. Fusaka-specific EIP-7623 token counting.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is EIP-7623 calldata floor formula correct?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Compute expected calldata_floor manually: tokens * 10 + 21000. 2. Compare with implementation's calldata_floor. 3. Verify FLOOR_CALLDATA_COST == 10. 4. Verify TX_BASE_COST == 21000. 5. Test with various calldata sizes and compositions.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0039. Boundary: tb-001. Fusaka-specific EIP-7623 floor formula.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is decode_transaction the inverse of encode_transaction?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. For each transaction type, create valid transaction object. 2. Encode transaction to bytes. 3. Decode bytes back to transaction object. 4. Verify decoded == original for all fields. 5. Test round-trip for adversarial field values.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0040. Boundary: tb-004. Non-bijective codec enables transaction manipulation.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does decode_transaction reject unknown type prefixes?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Send bytes with type prefix 0x05 and verify TransactionTypeError. 2. Test all invalid prefixes (0x05-0xFF). 3. Verify no silent acceptance of unknown types. 4. Verify proper error propagation (not IndexError, etc.). 5. Check boundary between RLP legacy and typed transactions.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0041. Boundary: tb-004. Unknown type acceptance causes undefined behavior.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is decode_transaction crash-resistant to malformed input?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Fuzz decode_transaction with random byte sequences. 2. Verify only TransactionTypeError or valid transaction returned. 3. Check no IndexError, OverflowError, MemoryError propagates. 4. Test truncated inputs, oversized inputs, malformed RLP. 5. Verify node stability under adversarial P2P traffic.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0042. Boundary: tb-004. Crash from P2P packet is critical DoS vulnerability.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Are EIP-7702 authorizations bound to chain_id and nonce?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create EIP-7702 authorization with specific chain_id and nonce. 2. Verify signing_hash_7702 includes chain_id and authorization data. 3. Attempt replay with different chain_id - verify rejection. 4. Attempt replay with stale nonce - verify rejection. 5. Verify nonce must match current authorizer account nonce.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0043. Boundary: tb-008. Fusaka-related EIP-7702. Stale auth replay enables unauthorized code delegation.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is transaction hash computation deterministic and unique?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Compute hash for legacy transaction: keccak256(rlp.encode(tx)). 2. Compute hash for typed transaction: keccak256(type_prefix || tx_data). 3. Verify same transaction produces same hash across multiple calls. 4. Verify different transactions produce different hashes. 5. Test for hash collisions across transaction types.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0044. Boundary: tb-001. Non-deterministic or colliding hashes break tx tracking.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does close_state release all references?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Call close_state after block finalization. 2. Verify _main_trie reference deleted. 3. Verify _storage_tries reference deleted. 4. Verify _snapshots reference deleted. 5. Verify created_accounts reference deleted. Check memory profile in long-running node.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0045. Boundary: tb-003. Memory leak affects long-running nodes.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does set_account_balance set (not add) the balance?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Set account balance to value X. 2. Call set_account_balance with value Y. 3. Verify balance == Y (not X + Y). 4. Test with coinbase payment code paths. 5. Verify no ether inflation from incorrect set semantics.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0046. Boundary: tb-003. Add instead of set causes ether inflation.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does set_code trigger empty account destruction?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Create account with only code (nonce==0, balance==0). 2. Call set_code with empty bytes. 3. Verify modify_state destroys the now-empty account. 4. Test with CREATE/CREATE2 deployment that fails. 5. Test EIP-7702 code delegation scenarios.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0047. Boundary: tb-003. Ghost accounts if destruction not triggered.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is EIP-155 signing hash correctly formed?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Compute signing_hash_155 for test transaction. 2. Verify hash == keccak256(rlp.encode([...fields, chain_id, 0, 0])). 3. Verify trailing zeros are included. 4. Attempt cross-chain replay of EIP-155 transaction. 5. Verify different chains produce different signing hashes.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0048. Boundary: tb-009. Broken EIP-155 enables cross-chain replay attacks.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is ECDSA non-repudiation property maintained?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify signing hash includes all transaction fields. 2. Verify no field can be modified without invalidating signature. 3. Verify transaction hash provides unique permanent identifier. 4. Review for any signing hash construction flaws. 5. This is primarily a cryptographic property verification.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0049. Boundary: tb-001. Non-repudiation violations require ECDSA breaks (theoretical).

checklist.title: Verify Conditional Scope: Are Fusaka gas economics consensus-safe under transaction ordering?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify TX_MAX_GAS_LIMIT bounds single transaction gas. 2. Verify calldata_floor is enforced regardless of ordering. 3. Test block building with various transaction orderings. 4. Compare results across client implementations. 5. Document MEV implications but note out-of-scope.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0050. CONDITIONAL SCOPE - MEV generally out-of-scope unless causing consensus failure.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Does Engine API enforce full transaction validation?
checklist.test_procedure: 1. Verify JWT authentication on Engine API endpoints. 2. Submit invalid block payload via Engine API. 3. Verify full EL transaction validation occurs. 4. Verify invalid transactions are rejected despite CL trust. 5. Test that compromised CL cannot bypass EL validation.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-0051. Boundary: tb-006. CL-EL boundary. Invalid block processing causes consensus failure.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Does get_beacon_proposer_index correctly use pre-computed proposer_lookahead instead of on-demand computation?

reachability.notes: If this function falls back to on-demand computation instead of using the lookahead, it would produce different results (since state may have changed since lookahead computation), causing consensus failures.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Locate the get_beacon_proposer_index function implementation. 2. Verify it accesses state.proposer_lookahead[state.slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH] directly. 3. Confirm there is NO fallback path to on-demand computation (compute_proposer_index or equivalent). 4. Create a test case where state changes between epoch boundary and slot execution. 5. Compare the proposer index returned by lookahead-based method vs. on-demand computation to verify they could diverge. 6. Verify all code paths through get_beacon_proposer_index use the lookahead array exclusively.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-36, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Invariant: FULU-SG005-INV-001. EIP-7917 modification requires strict use of pre-computed lookahead.

checklist.title: Verify Consensus Layer correctly populates proposer_lookahead at epoch boundaries

reachability.notes: Supporting check for actor-consensus-layer node coverage.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify where proposer_lookahead is computed and stored in state. 2. Verify computation occurs at epoch boundaries only (not per-slot). 3. Confirm the lookahead array has exactly SLOTS_PER_EPOCH entries. 4. Verify the computation uses the correct state snapshot (epoch boundary state). 5. Check that proposer_lookahead is immutable within an epoch.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-36, Supporting node: actor-consensus-layer

checklist.title: Verify Validator uses correct proposer index from lookahead for block proposal duties

reachability.notes: Supporting check for actor-validator node coverage.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Trace how validators determine their block proposal duties. 2. Verify validators query get_beacon_proposer_index which uses the lookahead. 3. Confirm validators do not perform independent on-demand computation. 4. Check that validators and consensus layer agree on proposer assignment for any given slot. 5. Test edge cases at epoch boundaries where lookahead transitions.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-36, Supporting node: actor-validator

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is fork upgrade irreversibility enforced across all state transitions at tb-007?

reachability.notes: Fork irreversibility is consensus-critical. A reversible fork would allow fork oscillation attacks.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify all code paths that invoke upgrade_to_fulu(state). 2. Verify that post-state always has fork.current_version == FULU_FORK_VERSION after upgrade. 3. Enumerate all state transition functions and verify none can revert fork.current_version to a pre-Fulu version. 4. Attempt to craft P2P messages that could trigger fork version reversion. 5. Check for race conditions or edge cases in fork scheduler that could cause fork oscillation. 6. Verify that beacon node rejects any state with inconsistent fork version history.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-3, Trust Boundary: tb-007, Invariant: fork-SG-001-INV-003. Fork oscillation attacks could cause network instability and consensus failure.

checklist.title: Supporting Check: Does actor-fork-scheduler correctly enforce one-way fork transitions?

reachability.notes: Fork irreversibility is consensus-critical. A reversible fork would allow fork oscillation attacks.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review fork scheduler logic for fork version ordering enforcement. 2. Verify that fork scheduler only permits forward transitions (e.g., DENEB -> FULU, never FULU -> DENEB). 3. Check that fork epoch boundaries are correctly calculated and enforced. 4. Test edge cases around epoch boundaries where fork transitions occur. 5. Verify scheduler rejects any attempt to schedule a downgrade.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-3, Supporting node: actor-fork-scheduler

checklist.title: Supporting Check: Does actor-beacon-node validate fork version monotonicity in received states?

reachability.notes: Fork irreversibility is consensus-critical. A reversible fork would allow fork oscillation attacks.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review beacon node state validation logic for fork version checks. 2. Verify that beacon node rejects any state where fork.current_version is less than expected for the given epoch. 3. Test P2P sync scenarios where a malicious peer sends states with reverted fork versions. 4. Check that state transitions within beacon node maintain fork version monotonicity. 5. Verify that checkpoint sync and snap sync correctly validate fork versions.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-3, Supporting node: actor-beacon-node

checklist.title: Can upgrade_to_fulu corrupt or drop BeaconState fields during Electra-to-Fulu fork transition?

reachability.notes: State preservation during fork is consensus-critical. Triggered automatically at fork epoch via P2P consensus.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Enumerate all fields in electra.BeaconState and identify which fields are explicitly modified by upgrade_to_fulu (expected: fork, proposer_lookahead). 2. Create a pre-fork BeaconState with known values for all fields. 3. Execute upgrade_to_fulu and capture the post-state. 4. For each field NOT in the explicit modification list, assert post.F == pre.F. 5. Check for subtle corruption vectors: (a) deep-copy vs shallow-copy issues for nested structures, (b) default value initialization that overwrites existing data, (c) missing field assignments in new struct initialization, (d) type conversion errors during SSZ serialization/deserialization. 6. Verify field preservation under edge cases: maximum-size validators list, full historical roots, boundary epoch values.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-4, Invariant: fork-SG-002-INV-001, Assumption: asm-009. This is consensus-critical - any field corruption would cause chain splits or fund loss.

checklist.title: Falsification Test: Can the fork version history chain be broken during upgrade_to_fulu transition?

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify the upgrade_to_fulu state transition function in the beacon node codebase. 2. Examine the implementation to verify that post.fork.previous_version is explicitly set to pre.fork.current_version. 3. Design test cases that exercise the upgrade transition with various pre-states, including edge cases (e.g., genesis fork, consecutive upgrades). 4. Verify the post-condition holds: assert(post.fork.previous_version == pre.fork.current_version). 5. Attempt to falsify by: (a) checking for any code path that could skip the version assignment, (b) testing with corrupted pre-state fork data, (c) verifying no race conditions exist in concurrent state transitions. 6. Validate that fork digest computation downstream correctly uses the maintained version chain.
checklist.falsification_strategy: Attempt to trigger upgrade_to_fulu with edge-case pre-states (empty version, mismatched versions, repeated upgrades) and verify whether the version chain invariant can be violated. Check for off-by-one errors in version array indexing.
checklist.impact_if_violated: Fork digest mismatch causing P2P network partition - nodes with broken version chains would compute different fork digests and fail to communicate with correct nodes.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-5, Invariant: fork-SG-002-INV-002, Assumption: asm-009. Bug bounty eligible with PoC showing fork version chain breakage.

Verify FULU_FORK_VERSION constant matches the expected fork version from the specification. 4. Create a test case that invokes upgrade_to_fulu and asserts post.fork.current_version == FULU_FORK_VERSION. 5. Test edge cases: verify the upgrade path from the previous fork (Electra) correctly transitions to Fulu. 6. Attempt to falsify by checking if any code path could set an incorrect version or skip the version assignment.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-6, Post-condition from invariant fork-SG-002-INV-003. Incorrect fork version after upgrade causes the node to become incompatible with the network, effectively creating a network partition. This is a critical post-condition that must hold after every fork upgrade operation.

Test with edge cases: empty states, maximum-size states, and states at fork boundaries.

checklist.title: Falsify Fork Version Branch Exclusivity: Can compute_fork_version return ambiguous or missing results for any epoch value?

Extract the epoch thresholds for each fork (e.g., GENESIS_EPOCH, ALTAIR_FORK_EPOCH, BELLATRIX_FORK_EPOCH, CAPELLA_FORK_EPOCH, DENEB_FORK_EPOCH, ELECTRA_FORK_EPOCH). 3. Test boundary epoch values: exactly at each fork epoch threshold, one below, one above. 4. Verify branch conditions are mutually exclusive: check that no epoch value satisfies multiple branch conditions simultaneously. 5. Verify branch conditions are exhaustive: check that every valid epoch value (0 to MAX_UINT64) satisfies exactly one branch condition. 6. Look for off-by-one errors in comparison operators (< vs <=, > vs >=). 7. Test with epoch = 0 (genesis) to ensure it returns GENESIS_FORK_VERSION. 8. Test with epoch = MAX_UINT64 to ensure the latest fork version is returned. 9. Verify the ordering of if-else branches matches the chronological order of forks to prevent earlier branches from shadowing later ones.

checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-22, Trust Boundary: tb-001, Invariant: p2p-SG-006-INV-001. This is a CRITICAL boundary check - any single REJECT bypass allows network-wide propagation of invalid sidecars.

checklist.title: Verify Gossip Network Actor (actor-gossip-network): Can an external attacker inject sidecars that bypass validation at the network boundary?

reachability.notes: An attacker sends crafted sidecars via gossip. Any bypass of a REJECT check is a critical vulnerability.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify the exact code path where gossip messages enter the system from the P2P network layer. 2. Review message deserialization for potential type confusion or buffer overflow vulnerabilities. 3. Verify that malformed gossip envelopes are rejected before validation pipeline processing. 4. Check for differences in validation between direct peer connections and relayed messages. 5. Test rate limiting and resource exhaustion protections at the gossip network boundary. 6. Verify that rejected messages do not leak information to attackers (timing, error messages).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-22, Supporting node check for actor-gossip-network. Focus on untrusted data entering from external peers.

checklist.title: Verify Beacon Node Actor (actor-beacon-node): Does the beacon node correctly enforce all REJECT conditions before forwarding sidecars?

reachability.notes: An attacker sends crafted sidecars via gossip. Any bypass of a REJECT check is a critical vulnerability.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Trace the message flow from gossip receipt to peer forwarding within the beacon node. 2. Identify the exact function(s) implementing each of the 13 REJECT checks. 3. Verify that the forwarding decision is made ONLY after ALL checks pass. 4. Check for any code paths that could forward messages before validation completes (async handling bugs). 5. Review state dependencies - ensure validation uses correct current state (head block, current slot). 6. Test behavior when beacon node is syncing - verify validation is not relaxed. 7. Check for TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) vulnerabilities between validation and forwarding.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-22, Supporting node check for actor-beacon-node. The beacon node is the trust boundary enforcer - any logic error here compromises network integrity.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Is the active validator set correctly filtered before proposer selection?

reachability.notes: If inactive validators are included in the selection pool, they could be chosen as proposers but would be unable to produce blocks.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Locate the get_beacon_proposer_indices function in the consensus layer implementation. 2. Verify that get_active_validator_indices(state, epoch) is called before any proposer selection logic. 3. Trace the validator set used in proposer computation and confirm it originates from get_active_validator_indices. 4. Attempt to inject an inactive validator (exited, slashed, or not yet activated) into the selection pool and verify it is rejected. 5. Write a test case that creates a state with mixed active/inactive validators and asserts only active validators appear in proposer indices.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-38, Invariant: FULU-SG006-INV-001, Trust Boundary: tb-009. Impact: Including inactive validators would cause missed slots when selected as proposers, degrading network liveness.

checklist.title: Verify Consensus Layer correctly obtains active validator indices for epoch-based proposer selection

reachability.notes: If inactive validators are included in the selection pool, they could be chosen as proposers but would be unable to produce blocks.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Review the get_active_validator_indices function implementation. 2. Verify it correctly checks validator activation_epoch <= epoch < exit_epoch. 3. Confirm slashed validators are excluded from the active set if required by spec. 4. Test edge cases: validators activating at current epoch, validators exiting at current epoch, validators with pending withdrawals. 5. Verify the epoch parameter is correctly passed from get_beacon_proposer_indices to get_active_validator_indices.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-38, Supporting node check for actor-consensus-layer. Ensures the active validator filtering logic is correct.

checklist.title: Verify Validator actor cannot influence proposer selection to include inactive validators

reachability.notes: If inactive validators are included in the selection pool, they could be chosen as proposers but would be unable to produce blocks.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify any validator-controlled inputs that flow into proposer selection logic. 2. Verify that validator attestations or block proposals cannot manipulate the active validator set used for proposer indices. 3. Check that state transitions updating validator status (activation, exit, slashing) correctly reflect in subsequent proposer computations. 4. Ensure no race conditions exist between validator status updates and proposer index calculations.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-38, Supporting node check for actor-validator. Ensures validators cannot manipulate proposer selection.

checklist.title: Can IGNORED sidecars be permanently discarded or forwarded without re-validation?

reachability.notes: Incorrect IGNORE handling could cause data loss (permanent discard) or amplification (forwarding).

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify the gossip validation handler for blob sidecars (p2p-SG-006). 2. Trace all code paths where IGNORE is returned as the validation result. 3. For each IGNORE path, verify that the sidecar is queued for later re-processing (not discarded). 4. Verify that IGNORED sidecars are NOT forwarded to other peers. 5. Attempt to trigger IGNORE conditions (e.g., sidecar arrives before its parent block) and confirm the sidecar is retained in a pending queue. 6. Verify the queue has proper bounds and eviction policies to prevent memory exhaustion. 7. Confirm that queued sidecars are re-validated when their dependencies become available.
checklist.falsification_strategy: Design a test that triggers an IGNORE condition and then verifies: (a) the sidecar is not permanently discarded by checking it remains accessible for later processing, and (b) the sidecar is not forwarded by monitoring outbound P2P messages. A successful violation would show either data loss (sidecar unavailable when needed) or message amplification (IGNORED sidecar propagated).
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W2B3-23, Invariant: p2p-SG-006-INV-002, Assumption: asm-007. Bug bounty requires PoC showing IGNORED sidecar being forwarded without re-validation.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity: Is proposer selection seed correctly domain-separated using DOMAIN_BEACON_PROPOSER?

reachability.notes: Incorrect domain would cause proposer selection to correlate with attestation committee selection or sync committee selection, reducing unpredictability.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Locate the proposer selection implementation that uses get_seed(). 2. Verify that get_seed() is called with exactly three arguments: state, epoch, and DOMAIN_BEACON_PROPOSER. 3. Check that DOMAIN_BEACON_PROPOSER is a distinct constant from other domain constants (DOMAIN_BEACON_ATTESTER, DOMAIN_SYNC_COMMITTEE, etc.). 4. Trace all call sites of get_seed() for proposer selection and confirm no alternative domain values are used. 5. Verify that no code paths exist where proposer selection bypasses domain-separated seed generation. 6. Test that changing the domain constant produces different proposer selections for the same epoch/state.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-39, Source Invariant: FULU-SG006-INV-002, Threat: stride-017, Trust Boundary: tb-009. Wrong domain separation would make proposer selection predictable or correlated with other selections, enabling MEV extraction and potential consensus manipulation.

checklist.title: Verify Domain Constant Uniqueness: Are all domain separation constants (DOMAIN_BEACON_PROPOSER, DOMAIN_BEACON_ATTESTER, etc.) guaranteed to be unique?

reachability.notes: Incorrect domain would cause proposer selection to correlate with attestation committee selection or sync committee selection, reducing unpredictability.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Enumerate all DOMAIN_* constants used in the codebase. 2. Verify each domain constant has a unique value that cannot collide with others. 3. Check that domain constants are defined as immutable/constant values. 4. Verify no dynamic computation or user input can influence domain values. 5. Confirm domain constants match the Ethereum consensus specification exactly.
checklist.notes: Supporting check for boundary edge tb-009. Domain collision would break the security guarantees of randomness separation.

checklist.title: Can process_epoch sub-process execution order be violated, causing state divergence across clients?

reachability.notes: Reordering epoch processing steps would cause different state after epoch transitions, leading to consensus failures. The proposer lookahead must be last because it depends on the updated validator set.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify all 16 epoch sub-processes within process_epoch (including EIP-7917's process_proposer_lookahead as the 16th step). 2. Map the dependency graph between sub-processes to understand which state mutations each step relies upon. 3. Verify that no code path allows sub-processes to execute out of order (e.g., through async calls, parallel execution, or conditional branches). 4. Create a test harness that artificially reorders sub-process execution and compare resulting beacon state hashes against correctly-ordered execution. 5. Specifically verify that process_proposer_lookahead executes AFTER all 15 prior steps complete, as it depends on the updated validator registry. 6. Check for any race conditions or concurrent access patterns that could cause non-deterministic ordering. 7. Validate that state mutations from each step are properly committed before the next step begins.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-40, Invariant: FULU-SG007-INV-001, Assumption: asm-014. This is a consensus-critical invariant - violation would cause chain splits between clients. Requires PoC showing state divergence when process steps are reordered.

checklist.title: Can process_proposer_lookahead execute before all 15 preceding steps in process_epoch complete?

reachability.notes: If process_proposer_lookahead runs before validator registry updates, it would compute the lookahead using stale validator data.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify the process_epoch function and verify it contains exactly 16 ordered steps. 2. Confirm process_proposer_lookahead is invoked as step 16 (the final step). 3. Trace data dependencies: verify process_proposer_lookahead reads from validator registry, randao mix, and other state that is modified by steps 1-15. 4. Attempt to invoke process_proposer_lookahead with pre-update state (before validator activations/exits are processed) and compare proposer assignments. 5. Check for any code paths (error handling, early returns, conditional branches) that could cause process_proposer_lookahead to execute before steps 1-15 complete. 6. Verify no asynchronous or parallel execution could allow out-of-order processing. 7. Create a PoC demonstrating proposer assignment divergence when step ordering is violated.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-41, Invariant: FULU-SG007-INV-002, Assumption: asm-014. This is an ordering invariant - the function must execute as step 16 after all validator registry updates. Bug bounty requires PoC showing proposer assignment error when ordering is violated.

checklist.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for tb-009: Is the proposer_lookahead shift operation correctly removing exactly SLOTS_PER_EPOCH entries?

reachability.notes: An off-by-one in the shift amount would leave stale entries at the front or remove entries for the current epoch, causing proposer assignment errors.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Locate the process_proposer_lookahead function implementation. 2. Verify the shift operation uses exactly SLOTS_PER_EPOCH as the offset (not SLOTS_PER_EPOCH - 1 or SLOTS_PER_EPOCH + 1). 3. Trace the array slice operation: confirm state.proposer_lookahead[:last_epoch_start] = state.proposer_lookahead[SLOTS_PER_EPOCH:] where last_epoch_start = len(state.proposer_lookahead) - SLOTS_PER_EPOCH. 4. Create test cases with boundary values: (a) shift by SLOTS_PER_EPOCH - 1 to verify stale entry detection, (b) shift by SLOTS_PER_EPOCH + 1 to verify array underflow handling. 5. Verify that after shift, the first entry corresponds to the correct epoch's first slot proposer.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-42, Trust Boundary: tb-009, Invariant: FULU-SG008-INV-001. Shifting by wrong amount causes proposer assignment errors: SLOTS_PER_EPOCH - 1 leaves stale entries, SLOTS_PER_EPOCH + 1 causes underflow or missing proposers.

checklist.title: Verify Consensus Layer proposer_lookahead state consistency during epoch transitions

reachability.notes: An off-by-one in the shift amount would leave stale entries at the front or remove entries for the current epoch, causing proposer assignment errors.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify all code paths in actor-consensus-layer that invoke process_proposer_lookahead. 2. Verify that the proposer_lookahead array length remains consistent across epoch transitions (should be exactly EPOCHS_AHEAD * SLOTS_PER_EPOCH after each transition). 3. Confirm that the consensus layer correctly handles edge cases: first epoch, epoch rollover, and post-finalization. 4. Trace data flow from consensus layer to validator actor to ensure proposer assignments are derived from correct lookahead entries.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-42, Covered Node: actor-consensus-layer. Supporting check for boundary integrity.

checklist.title: Verify Validator receives correct proposer assignments after lookahead shift

reachability.notes: An off-by-one in the shift amount would leave stale entries at the front or remove entries for the current epoch, causing proposer assignment errors.

checklist.test_procedure: 1. Identify how actor-validator queries or receives proposer assignments from the lookahead array. 2. Verify that the validator correctly maps slot numbers to lookahead array indices after a shift operation. 3. Test proposer duty assignment at epoch boundary slots (first slot of new epoch, last slot of old epoch). 4. Confirm that if a shift error occurs, the validator would detect stale or missing proposer data rather than silently using incorrect assignments.
checklist.notes: Source: PROP-W1B10-42, Covered Node: actor-validator. Supporting check for boundary integrity.
